name: Natural Reserve Laguna de Gallocanta – running circular from Bello
name_original: Reserva Natural Laguna de Gallocanta – running circular desde Bello
region_intro: Jogging routes around Bello are primarily defined by the unique landscape surrounding the Natural Reserve Laguna de Gallocanta. This region sits on a high continental plain at nearly 1,000 meters of altitude, offering paths that encircle Europe's largest saltwater lagoon. The terrain around the lagoon is generally flat to moderate, while the broader area is encircled by the Iberian System mountain ranges. Best jogging routes around Bello The most popular jogging route is Natural Reserve Laguna de Gallocanta – running circular from Bello, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route encircles the significant Laguna de Gallocanta. Another top favourite among local runners is Valdellosa loop from Icona, a difficult 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This route features more significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced runners. Local runners also love the Running loop from Blancas, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 32 minutes. Jogging around Bello is defined by the tranquil Laguna de Gallocanta, high continental plains, and views towards the Iberian System mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging running routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore Bello's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Bello?
A: Komoot features several running routes around Bello, offering diverse options for joggers. You can find a selection of top-rated trails in this guide.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes around Bello?
A: The terrain around Bello is primarily defined by the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laguna_de_Gallocanta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Reserve Laguna de Gallocanta&lt;/a&gt;. Paths encircling the lagoon are generally flat to moderate, making them suitable for various fitness levels. The broader region also includes views towards the Iberian System mountain ranges, suggesting varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Bello?
A: Yes, there are options for beginners. An easier, shorter option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Blancas&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.4 km long and ideal for any fitness level, typically completed in around 32 minutes.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Bello area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running trails around Bello are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983369951/natural-reserve-laguna-de-gallocanta-running-circular-from-bello" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Reserve Laguna de Gallocanta – running circular from Bello&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.4 km long.

Q: Are the running trails around Bello scenic? What can I see?
A: Yes, the trails are very scenic. Jogging around Bello offers tranquil views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Gallocanta&lt;/a&gt;, Europe's largest saltwater lagoon, set against a high continental plain. You can also enjoy views towards the Iberian System mountain ranges. The lagoon is also a significant spot for birdwatching, especially during crane migrations.

Q: Are there any more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a greater challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31447344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdellosa loop from Icona&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.3 km path with more significant elevation gain, requiring good to very good fitness.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Bello?
A: The Laguna de Gallocanta is a crucial stopover for thousands of cranes during their migrations between autumn and spring. This makes these seasons particularly exceptional for combining jogging with wildlife observation. However, the flat to moderate terrain around the lagoon is inviting for joggers of different skill levels year-round.

Q: Are there options for parking near the jogging routes in Bello?
A: While specific parking facilities for each route are not detailed, the trails around Bello are generally accessible. Given the rural nature of the area, parking is often available near trailheads or in the nearby villages.

Q: What makes the running experience in Bello unique?
A: The unique aspect of running in Bello is its tranquil setting around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Gallocanta&lt;/a&gt;. This internationally important wetland offers picturesque natural scenery and exceptional birdwatching opportunities, especially during crane migrations, making for a truly distinctive outdoor experience.

Q: Can I combine running with birdwatching in Bello?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Gallocanta&lt;/a&gt; is a crucial stopover for thousands of cranes during their migrations. Observatories like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reguera Observatory (Gallocanta Lagoon)&lt;/a&gt; around the lagoon offer privileged views, allowing you to combine your run with wildlife observation.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions I can see while running in Bello?
A: Yes, while jogging around Bello, you can encounter several points of interest. Besides the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; itself, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Good Agreement&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reguera Observatory&lt;/a&gt;, both offering unique sights along your route.

Q: Are the running trails in Bello suitable for families?
A: Yes, the generally flat to moderate terrain around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Gallocanta&lt;/a&gt; makes many of the paths suitable for families. Shorter, easier loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Blancas&lt;/a&gt; are great options for a family outing.
tips: Picturesque and very quiet, this gravel track that runs next to the Gallocanta Lagoon is a real pleasure for runners, hikers and cyclists alike. The views are very beautiful and the landscape very pleasant.
